channel.title: Digital Assets Morning Wire
channel.description: Daily digital-asset regulatory and market digest. AI-sourced and AI-generated — verify against official sources before acting.

item.title: Sen. Wyden urges Senate leaders to preserve contested blockchain developer protections in broader crypto bill
item.description: United States Senator Ron Wyden wrote to Senate leaders urging them to keep a provision in a broad cryptocurrency bill that would shield developers of non-custodial blockchain software from being classified as money transmitters. So what: A carve-out exempting non-custodial software developers from money-transmitter status would narrow where United States anti-money-laundering registration and controls apply, a distinction relevant when assessing digital-asset service providers and counterparties. [single source]

item.title: Binance Wallet adds Plume’s yield vault offering access to Invesco and Bitwise funds
item.description: Binance Wallet added access to a tokenised yield product from Plume that routes users into tokenised versions of an Invesco government-securities fund and a Bitwise fund, each yielding roughly 3.5 percent. So what: Wallets channelling retail users into tokenised real-world-asset funds blur the line between crypto platforms and regulated fund distribution, a pattern worth tracking when assessing how tokenised securities reach customers across jurisdictions. [single source]

item.title: European Commission looks to expand MiCA to cover emergence of tokenization, non-EU stablecoin issuers: report
item.description: The European Commission is considering widening its Markets in Crypto-Assets Regulation (MiCA) to cover tokenised securities and stablecoin issuers based outside the European Union, and has opened a stakeholder comment period that closes on 30 September 2026. So what: Extending the European Union's crypto rulebook to reach non-European-Union stablecoin issuers and tokenised securities could pull firms serving European customers from abroad into its compliance perimeter, relevant for cross-border product and correspondent-relationship assessments. [single source]

item.title: Cardano founding entity EMURGO steps down from Pentad governance role after wallet exploit
item.description: EMURGO, one of the founding organisations of the Cardano blockchain, withdrew from Cardano's Pentad governance group to focus on reimbursing users after a flaw in its SecondFi wallet let attackers take about 16 million ADA tokens (roughly 2.4 million United States dollars) from 374 wallets. So what: Wallet-software vulnerabilities that drain customer funds underline custody and technology risk in self-custody products, a consideration when evaluating exposure to crypto service providers and the operational resilience of digital-asset counterparties. [single source]

item.title: Adam Back's bitcoin treasury firm scraps SPAC merger, seeks new deal
item.description: Bitcoin Standard Treasury Company, a bitcoin-holding venture backed by Blockstream's Adam Back, scrapped the original terms of its planned merger with a Cantor-linked shell company, cancelled a private financing round of up to 1.5 billion United States dollars, postponed its shareholder vote, and is now seeking a new deal. So what: The unravelling of a planned public listing for a bitcoin-treasury company illustrates the funding and execution risk around firms whose balance sheets are concentrated in cryptoassets, relevant when assessing the financial stability of such counterparties. [single source]

item.title: Citadel abandons multi-year crypto lawsuit to focus on bankruptcy order against an ex-employee
item.description: Citadel Securities dropped its nearly three-year United States trade-secrets lawsuit against Swiss crypto trading firm Portofino Technologies and instead asked England's High Court to declare co-founder Leonard Lancia bankrupt over an unpaid arbitration award of about 6 million pounds. So what: Pursuing a civil judgment through cross-border bankruptcy proceedings shows how disputes involving crypto trading firms and their principals can move between jurisdictions, relevant when tracing the assets and legal exposure of individuals and firms in the sector. [single source]

item.title: India central bank seeks to bar financial institutions from exposure to crypto assets: Reuters
item.description: The Reserve Bank of India reaffirmed a policy stance leaning towards prohibition and, according to a Reuters report, wants banks and other financial institutions barred from holding, trading, or otherwise taking on exposure to cryptoassets. So what: A central bank moving to prohibit regulated financial institutions from any cryptoasset exposure signals a hard-line prudential approach in a major market, relevant when calibrating group-wide risk appetite and cross-border activity involving jurisdictions that restrict bank crypto exposure. [single source]

item.title: Reserve Bank of India still favors crypto prohibition to curtail tax evasion: Reuters
item.description: According to government documents reviewed by Reuters, India's central bank, the Reserve Bank of India, told the government it continues to favour prohibiting cryptocurrency and opposes banks holding crypto assets or the use of private and rupee-pegged stablecoins, citing tax-evasion, financial-stability and capital-flight risks. So what: A major economy's central bank maintaining a prohibition stance means institutions with Indian exposure should expect continued restrictions on cryptoasset and stablecoin activity there, and cross-border virtual-asset flows involving India warrant heightened monitoring for tax-evasion and capital-flight typologies. [single source]

item.title: Nigel Farage to quit UK Parliament, seek new mandate amid probes into crypto-linked gifts
item.description: Nigel Farage, leader of the United Kingdom's Reform UK party, said he will resign his House of Commons seat and stand again in the resulting by-election, following two parliamentary standards investigations into his failure to declare cryptocurrency-linked gifts and support from crypto-industry figures. So what: The case shows how undisclosed benefits from cryptocurrency-industry figures create financial-crime and reputational exposure, reinforcing the value of enhanced due diligence on politically exposed persons with crypto-sector connections. [single source]

item.title: Man Serving Federal Prison Sentence Charged with Theft of Forfeited Cryptocurrency
item.description: The United States Department of Justice charged Rossen G. Iossifov, a Bulgarian national already serving a 111-month federal sentence for online-auction fraud, with conspiring in January 2024 to move roughly 290,000 United States dollars in cryptocurrency that the government had seized and forfeited, allegedly routing it through multiple exchanges and mixing services to launder it. So what: The alleged use of cryptocurrency exchanges and mixing services to move already-forfeited criminal proceeds highlights the mixer and layering typologies that virtual-asset transaction-monitoring controls are expected to detect and report. [official]

item.title: Circular to licensed corporations and SFC-licensed virtual asset service providers Implementing (i) robust authentication methods to reduce and mitigate hacking risks from phishing attacks and (ii) adequate monitoring and surveillance measures to identify suspicious activities
item.description: The Hong Kong Securities and Futures Commission (SFC) issued a circular directing licensed brokers and licensed virtual-asset service providers to strengthen customer login security against phishing attacks and to put in place monitoring and surveillance measures that can identify suspicious account activity. So what: Compliance teams overseeing Hong Kong virtual-asset business should expect the regulator to treat phishing-resistant customer authentication and suspicious-activity monitoring as a baseline supervisory standard and confirm that in-scope platforms and brokers are on track to meet it. [corroborated]

item.title: Bitwise says bitcoin’s ‘floor is rising’ despite AI boom and regulatory delays
item.description: The asset manager Bitwise published commentary arguing that bitcoin's price 'floor' is rising with each market cycle as ownership shifts from retail speculators to institutional investors, noting the current roughly 50 percent price decline is milder than the 78 percent and 84 percent falls seen in past downturns. So what: This is market commentary with no regulatory or enforcement content and limited direct relevance to financial-crime compliance beyond general awareness of growing institutional participation in cryptoasset markets. [single source]

item.title: Coinbase Chief Legal Officer Paul Grewal steps down, remains in advisory role
item.description: The cryptocurrency exchange Coinbase announced that its Chief Legal Officer, Paul Grewal, will step down on 31 July 2026 after about six years and move to a three-month advisory role, with Molly Abraham expected to become general counsel. So what: A senior legal leadership change at a major cryptocurrency exchange is largely a corporate-governance matter with limited direct financial-crime-compliance impact, though counterparties may watch for continuity in the firm's regulatory and legal posture. [single source]

item.title: Grayscale's CFO exits after 7 years with crypto asset manager
item.description: Grayscale, a United States digital-asset investment manager, said its chief financial officer stepped down after seven years for personal reasons and named two interim co-chief-financial-officers in his place. So what: Senior-finance turnover at a large digital-asset manager is a routine governance signal rather than a regulatory event, but it is worth noting when assessing the stability of fund providers or counterparties in the virtual-asset sector. [single source]

item.title: With SEC fight over, Coinbase's top legal exec Grewal moves on, and others reassigned
item.description: Coinbase, a United States cryptocurrency exchange, said its chief legal officer is departing now that its legal dispute with the United States Securities and Exchange Commission has ended, and it promoted a new general counsel and named a new vice chairman to reshuffle its legal leadership. So what: Legal-leadership changes at a major exchange after the close of a landmark regulatory case point to a more settled United States enforcement posture, which is relevant when reassessing the regulatory-risk profile of large virtual-asset counterparties. [single source]

item.title: Newest version of crypto Clarity Act may drop as soon as next week, sources say
item.description: Citing unnamed sources, CoinDesk reported that United States senators may release a new draft of the Digital Asset Market Clarity Act, a bill setting market-structure rules for cryptocurrency, as soon as the week of 16 July 2026, with possible Senate floor action the following week, though a dispute over ethics rules barring senior officials from crypto business ties remains unresolved. So what: Progress on comprehensive United States cryptocurrency market-structure legislation would clarify how digital-asset firms are supervised and could reshape compliance expectations for institutions dealing with United States-based cryptoasset businesses, warranting monitoring as the draft advances. [single source]

item.title: Billions flowing out of bitcoin ETFs and private credit funds suggest rising market risks
item.description: Market commentators reported that roughly five billion United States dollars flowed out of bitcoin exchange-traded funds and about fifteen billion United States dollars in redemption requests hit private credit funds during the second quarter of 2026, alongside a fall of around fourteen percent in bitcoin's price, which they read as a sign of rising market-liquidity stress. So what: Simultaneous outflows across bitcoin funds and private credit indicate broader liquidity pressure, which is relevant for monitoring redemption and funding risk in any virtual-asset investment products held or serviced. [single source]

item.title: White House defends Trump’s regulatory appointments as CFTC vacancies complicate crypto bill push
item.description: The White House sent a letter to United States Senate leaders defending its record on filling financial-regulator posts, as unfilled seats at the Commodity Futures Trading Commission, now down to a single commissioner, slow progress on proposed cryptocurrency market-structure legislation known as the Clarity Act. So what: Delays in staffing the United States Commodity Futures Trading Commission hold back the market-structure rulemaking that would set federal oversight of digital assets, prolonging the current period of United States regulatory uncertainty for firms tracking those rules. [single source]

item.title: JPMorgan says bitcoin’s main risk isn’t Strategy, but blockchain adoption that doesn’t benefit public chains and tokens
item.description: Analysts at the United States bank JPMorgan argued in a research note that bitcoin's main long-term risk is not asset sales by the company Strategy but the prospect of mainstream financial institutions adopting private, permissioned blockchains that bypass public cryptocurrency networks. So what: A large bank's view that tokenisation and settlement may shift onto permissioned rather than public blockchains is relevant for anticipating where institutional digital-asset activity, and the financial-crime controls attached to it, will actually take place. [single source]

item.title: Why this Wall Street veteran is betting on Avalanche
item.description: In a podcast interview, the chief executive of Avalanche Treasury Company, a former Wall Street executive, laid out his case for investing in the Avalanche blockchain and its AVAX token through a listed vehicle designed to give regulated, long-term exposure to that ecosystem. So what: The rise of listed treasury vehicles offering regulated exposure to a specific blockchain is a market-structure trend worth tracking when assessing new forms of institutional digital-asset exposure. [single source]

item.title: Supreme Court ruling expanding Trump’s authority over federal agencies raises questions for SEC, CFTC as crypto rulemaking advances
item.description: The United States Supreme Court ruled six to three to broaden the president's power to remove leaders of independent federal agencies, a decision that legal observers say could make cryptocurrency rules written by the Securities and Exchange Commission and the Commodity Futures Trading Commission easier for future administrations to reverse. So what: A ruling that weakens the independence of United States financial regulators raises the risk that today's industry-friendly digital-asset rules could be undone later, which matters for the durability of any United States regulatory framework that compliance planning relies on. [single source]

item.title: Hong Kong SFC orders crypto platforms, online brokers to phase out OTP logins
item.description: The Block reported that the Hong Kong Securities and Futures Commission (SFC) is requiring licensed virtual-asset trading platforms and online brokers to phase out one-time passwords for customer logins in favour of stronger authentication such as passkeys, and to add monitoring that flags suspicious logins, trades and withdrawals. So what: Compliance functions covering Hong Kong virtual-asset and online-brokerage clients should track the shift away from one-time-password logins toward phishing-resistant authentication and the buildout of account-activity monitoring, since these are becoming the regulator's expected defences against account-takeover fraud. [single source]

item.title: Crypto hack losses fall below $1 billion in H1 2026 despite record attack volume: Immunefi
item.description: A blockchain-security firm reported that cryptocurrency theft losses fell below one billion United States dollars, to about 972 million, in the first half of 2026 even as the number of attacks hit a record 207, with attackers shifting away from smart-contract flaws toward stolen private keys and infrastructure failures. So what: The persistently high volume of thefts, now driven more by stolen private keys and infrastructure compromise than code flaws, keeps the proceeds of virtual-asset hacks a live concern for transaction monitoring and blocked-address screening at institutions handling digital-asset flows. [single source]

item.title: CFTC Chair Selig warns regulators will end up ‘writing all the rules’ for crypto if Clarity Act stalls
item.description: The chair of the United States Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) warned that his agency and the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) would end up writing cryptocurrency market rules on their own if Congress fails to pass the pending Clarity Act market-structure bill before the Senate's August recess. So what: Continued uncertainty over which United States regulator oversees crypto markets, and whether the rules arrive via legislation or unilateral agency action, complicates forward compliance planning for firms with United States digital-asset exposure and warrants close monitoring of the bill's progress. [single source]

item.title: Swift rolls out new blockchain ledger to bring 24/7 banking to 17 global giants
item.description: The interbank messaging network Swift announced a shared blockchain ledger, now ready for live testing with seventeen major global banks including HSBC, that would let member banks move tokenised customer deposits for round-the-clock cross-border payments alongside existing settlement systems. So what: A bank-operated tokenised-deposit rail spanning major global institutions signals that on-chain payment settlement is moving into regulated banking infrastructure, raising forward-looking questions about transaction monitoring, travel-rule data capture and settlement-finality controls for tokenised money. [single source]

item.title: Sony secures conditional approval to set up U.S. stablecoin trust bank
item.description: The banking arm of Sony received preliminary conditional approval from the United States Office of the Comptroller of the Currency (OCC) to set up a national trust bank, to be called Connectia Trust and capitalised at forty million United States dollars, to manage dollar-denominated stablecoins, though it cannot begin operations until further regulatory approvals are granted. So what: Another major corporate entrant winning a United States trust-bank charter for stablecoin activity indicates that regulated, bank-grade stablecoin issuance is broadening, which is relevant to counterparty due diligence and reserve-backing assessments for institutions exposed to dollar-denominated stablecoins. [single source]

item.title: Circular to Licensed Corporations, SFC-licensed Virtual Asset Service Providers and Associated Entities - Cybersecurity Webinar
item.description: The Hong Kong Securities and Futures Commission issued a circular inviting licensed corporations, licensed virtual-asset service providers and their associated entities to a cybersecurity webinar, following its earlier guidance on defending against artificial-intelligence-enabled cyberattacks. So what: The webinar underlines the regulator's continuing supervisory focus on cybersecurity, and firms overseeing Hong Kong licensed or virtual-asset operations may wish to have relevant staff attend and use it to review their cyber controls. [official]

item.title: Crypto fatwa sparks debate over Pakistan’s digital asset framework
item.description: Pakistan's virtual-asset regulator met a senior Islamic scholar after he issued a religious ruling declaring cryptocurrency purchases impermissible under Islamic law and treating cryptoassets, including stablecoins, as non-wealth, intensifying debate over the country's planned digital-asset framework. So what: Religious rulings shaping how a large market treats cryptoassets and stablecoins add uncertainty to Pakistan's regulatory direction, relevant when assessing cross-border digital-asset activity and stablecoin acceptance involving that jurisdiction. [single source]

item.title: Michael Saylor posts cryptic Strategy chart after $216 million bitcoin sale: ‘Orange dots tell only part of the story’
item.description: Michael Saylor, executive chairman of the corporate bitcoin holder Strategy, posted a cryptic bitcoin-tracker chart after the company's largest-ever bitcoin sale of about 216 million United States dollars, while the firm still holds roughly 843,775 bitcoin. So what: This is corporate and market-sentiment commentary with no direct compliance angle, useful only as background on the largest corporate bitcoin holder's shift from accumulating toward selling. [single source]

item.title: Signs of life?: State of Crypto
item.description: A CoinDesk policy newsletter surveys United States crypto legislation, reporting that a combined draft of the Digital Asset Market Clarity Act market-structure bill is expected this week and that a ban on a central bank digital currency was recently enacted through a housing law. So what: Progress on United States market-structure legislation would reshape how digital-asset firms are supervised, so the combined bill's contents are worth monitoring as they emerge, though nothing is yet final. [single source]

item.title: Bitcoin is nearing a power law support line Fidelity has tracked since 2015
item.description: A CoinDesk article relays a Fidelity strategist's view that bitcoin is nearing a roughly 58,000 United States dollar support level predicted by a long-running price model, describing the area as an accumulation zone while noting no clear catalyst yet for a rebound. So what: This is market and technical-analysis commentary with no regulatory or compliance content, useful only as general market-context background. [single source]

item.title: Lending protocol Bonzo loses 77% of value locked as $9 million oracle exploit rattles Hedera
item.description: Bonzo Lend, a lending protocol on the Hedera blockchain, lost about nine million United States dollars after an attacker exploited a flaw in a third-party price oracle to manipulate token prices and borrow far more than the collateral supported, and the protocol's locked value fell 77 percent. So what: Freshly stolen exploit proceeds typically move quickly through mixing services and cross-chain bridges, so screening-provider alerts for exposure to the stolen funds are worth watching in the days that follow. [single source]

item.title: Bitcoin, ether ETFs snap eight-week outflow streaks with $282 million combined inflow
item.description: Spot bitcoin and ether exchange-traded funds recorded a combined net inflow of about 282 million United States dollars, ending an eight-week run of outflows. So what: Renewed fund inflows after a sustained outflow streak signal shifting institutional sentiment, useful context for the volumes moving through banks' brokerage, custody and payments relationships. [single source]

item.title: Bitcoin treasury company Empery Digital sold about half of its BTC stack
item.description: Bitcoin-treasury company Empery Digital sold 1,400 bitcoin for about 87 million United States dollars to help fund a stake in a United States data-centre facility, and said it has no plans to accumulate more bitcoin. So what: A bitcoin-treasury company pivoting from accumulation to selling reflects the funding and concentration risk around firms whose balance sheets are heavy in cryptoassets, relevant when assessing the financial stability of such counterparties. [single source]

item.title: Housing bill that includes a CBDC ban passed into law without Trump’s signature
item.description: A United States housing law took effect on 11 July 2026 without the President's signature and includes a provision barring the Federal Reserve from issuing a central bank digital currency. So what: A statutory bar on a United States central bank digital currency removes one potential future form of public digital money, though it carries little immediate operational effect for compliance functions given no such currency was in development. [single source]

item.title: DOJ to drop charges against man accused in $722 million BitClub crypto Ponzi scheme: Bloomberg
item.description: The United States Department of Justice is moving to dismiss its long-running fraud case against the alleged architect of the BitClub Network cryptocurrency scheme, which took about 722 million United States dollars from investors, after his legal team lobbied senior department officials. So what: The abandonment of a major cryptocurrency-fraud prosecution is a notable shift in United States enforcement posture, relevant when judging how vigorously past digital-asset fraud cases are now being pursued. [single source]

item.title: Here’s what’s next for the Clarity Act as Congress returns to Washington
item.description: The Block outlines the next steps for the United States Digital Asset Market Clarity Act, a cryptocurrency market-structure bill the House of Representatives passed about a year ago, as Senate committees merge their versions ahead of a possible floor vote before the August recess, with developer-liability and official-ethics provisions still unresolved. So what: Comprehensive United States market-structure legislation would clarify how digital-asset firms are supervised and reshape compliance expectations for institutions dealing with United States cryptoasset businesses, warranting monitoring as the bill advances. [single source]

item.title: Kraken to relaunch its mobile app with agentic trading and advice at the center
item.description: The cryptocurrency exchange Kraken said it will relaunch its mobile app built around artificial-intelligence "agentic" features that monitor markets and suggest trades matched to a user's goals, though the assistant will not execute trades itself. So what: Exchanges embedding automated trading suggestions raise suitability, advice and consumer-protection questions worth noting when assessing how exchange counterparties interact with retail customers. [single source]

item.title: U.S. government digital dollar set to be banned tonight under housing law's CBDC limit
item.description: A United States housing law containing a four-year ban on the Federal Reserve issuing a digital dollar took effect in mid-July 2026 without the President's signature, with the restriction set to expire at the end of 2030. So what: The temporary statutory bar on a United States central bank digital currency settles one question about the future of public digital money, with limited near-term operational impact given no such currency was under active development. [single source]

item.title: Binance co-CEO says 70% of EU withdrawals went to self-custody after MiCA deadline, with just 30% going to licensed platforms
item.description: A Binance co-chief-executive said that after the exchange suspended European Union services ahead of the 1 July Markets in Crypto-Assets Regulation deadline, about 70 percent of European users' withdrawals moved to self-custody wallets and only 30 percent to licensed platforms, questioning whether the rules reduced user risk. So what: A large migration of users to self-custody wallets that sit outside regulated exchanges' anti-money-laundering and know-your-customer controls is a monitoring blind spot worth factoring into oversight of European digital-asset flows. [single source]

item.title: ‘Communication challenge, nothing more’: Standard Chartered keeps $100,000 bitcoin target, calls Strategy selloff a signaling problem
item.description: A Standard Chartered analyst reaffirmed a 100,000 United States dollar end-of-2026 bitcoin price target, describing recent bitcoin sales by the company Strategy as a communication problem rather than a sign of financial distress. So what: Bank research maintaining a bullish bitcoin target amid a large holder's selling is market commentary rather than a regulatory event, useful only as sentiment context. [single source]

item.title: Circle soars after securing U.S. trust bank approval in crypto expansion
item.description: The United States Office of the Comptroller of the Currency granted stablecoin issuer Circle final approval to open a national trust bank that will provide digital-asset custody, initially for Circle and its affiliates, and the company's shares rose sharply on the news. So what: A dedicated, federally chartered trust bank for a major stablecoin issuer's custody activity broadens regulated, bank-grade digital-asset custody, relevant to counterparty due diligence and reserve-safekeeping assessments for institutions exposed to dollar-denominated stablecoins. [single source]

item.title: Stablecoin firm Circle wins final OCC approval to open national trust bank
item.description: The United States Office of the Comptroller of the Currency gave stablecoin issuer Circle final approval to establish a national trust bank, legally named First National Digital Currency Bank and operating as Circle National Trust, which at launch will provide fiduciary digital-asset custody for Circle and its affiliates and cannot take cash deposits or make loans. So what: Final approval of a national trust-bank charter dedicated to a leading stablecoin issuer's custody activity signals that bank-grade, federally supervised digital-asset custody is expanding, which matters for counterparty and safekeeping-arrangement assessments involving dollar-denominated stablecoins. [corroborated]

item.title: New Hampshire’s $100 million bitcoin bond proposal fails to pass final vote
item.description: New Hampshire's Executive Council voted three to two to reject a proposed 100 million United States dollar bitcoin-backed bond linked to a mining company, a scheme the state governor had backed. So what: The rejection of a state-level bitcoin-bond scheme shows continued caution among United States sub-national governments toward putting public money into cryptoassets, a modest signal on the pace of official crypto adoption. [single source]

item.title: DOJ charges federal inmate over alleged theft of $290,000 in crypto forfeited to US government
item.description: The Block reported on United States Department of Justice charges against a Bulgarian national already serving a federal fraud sentence, accused of moving about 290,000 United States dollars in government-forfeited cryptocurrency through exchanges and mixing services in January 2024 to launder it. So what: The alleged laundering of already-forfeited criminal proceeds through mixing services and exchanges illustrates the layering typologies that virtual-asset transaction-monitoring controls are expected to detect and report. [single source]

item.title: Cathie Wood’s Ark Invest buys $14 million worth of Circle, offloads Robinhood shares
item.description: The investment manager Ark Invest bought about 13.7 million United States dollars of shares in stablecoin issuer Circle across three of its exchange-traded funds while selling roughly 9.8 million United States dollars of Robinhood shares, as part of routine portfolio rebalancing. So what: Routine fund rebalancing into a listed stablecoin issuer is market-investment activity with no direct compliance implication, useful only as a signal of institutional interest in crypto-linked equities. [single source]

item.title: UK Labour MPs push to permanently ban crypto political donations
item.description: United Kingdom Labour members of Parliament are pushing amendments to an elections bill that would permanently bar political parties and candidates from accepting cryptocurrency donations, citing money-laundering and anonymous-donation risks after a controversy over a large crypto gift to another party's leader. So what: A move to prohibit cryptocurrency political donations reflects growing official concern about the traceability and source of crypto funds, reinforcing the enhanced due diligence expected around politically exposed persons with cryptoasset links. [single source]

item.title: Blockchain Evidence in Court: A Framework for Investigators
item.description: The blockchain-analytics firm TRM Labs published a practitioner guide on presenting blockchain evidence in court, advising investigators to separate factual testimony from expert opinion and to organise evidence into on-chain data, analytical conclusions and attribution. So what: The framework is a useful reference for strengthening how digital-asset investigation findings are documented so they withstand legal challenge, relevant to teams whose crypto-tracing work may support law-enforcement referrals or litigation. [single source]

item.title: Cyber-related Designations; Cuba Designations; Issuance of Cuba-related Frequently Asked Question
item.description: Cyber-related Designations; Cuba Designations; Issuance of Cuba-related Frequently Asked Question So what: Review the source directly; automated analysis unavailable. [official]

item.title: Binance.US CEO says exchange is rebuilding, eyes return to 20% U.S. market share
item.description: Binance.US CEO says exchange is rebuilding, eyes return to 20% U.S. market share So what: Review the source directly; automated analysis unavailable. [single source]

item.title: Trump's crypto riches loom over Clarity Act talks to ban conflicts for U.S. officials
item.description: Trump's crypto riches loom over Clarity Act talks to ban conflicts for U.S. officials So what: Review the source directly; automated analysis unavailable. [single source]

item.title: UK lays out tokenized finance roadmap with projected £33 billion annual boost
item.description: UK lays out tokenized finance roadmap with projected £33 billion annual boost So what: Review the source directly; automated analysis unavailable. [single source]

item.title: Mizuho says Circle bank approval doesn't solve USDC growth, stablecoin competition risks
item.description: Mizuho says Circle bank approval doesn't solve USDC growth, stablecoin competition risks So what: Review the source directly; automated analysis unavailable. [single source]

item.title: President Trump, White House press Senate to advance the Clarity Act while ethics fight looms
item.description: President Trump, White House press Senate to advance the Clarity Act while ethics fight looms So what: Review the source directly; automated analysis unavailable. [single source]

item.title: Chinese prosecutors call for more proactive approach to investigating crypto money laundering
item.description: Chinese prosecutors call for more proactive approach to investigating crypto money laundering So what: Review the source directly; automated analysis unavailable. [single source]

item.title: Bolivia weighs adding Tether's USDT to its national payments system
item.description: Bolivia weighs adding Tether's USDT to its national payments system So what: Review the source directly; automated analysis unavailable. [single source]

item.title: UK Treasury report on tokenization cites Ripple as convergence model
item.description: UK Treasury report on tokenization cites Ripple as convergence model So what: Review the source directly; automated analysis unavailable. [single source]

item.title: Courtroom-Ready Analytics: How Chainalysis Met the Daubert Standard
item.description: Courtroom-Ready Analytics: How Chainalysis Met the Daubert Standard So what: Review the source directly; automated analysis unavailable. [single source]

item.title: Michael Saylor’s Strategy added $467 million in cash, made no changes to bitcoin holdings
item.description: Michael Saylor’s Strategy added $467 million in cash, made no changes to bitcoin holdings So what: Review the source directly; automated analysis unavailable. [single source]

item.title: BlackRock, Goldman Sachs, JPMorgan, Morgan Stanley join UK government's tokenization taskforce
item.description: BlackRock, Goldman Sachs, JPMorgan, Morgan Stanley join UK government's tokenization taskforce So what: Review the source directly; automated analysis unavailable. [single source]

item.title: Resurgent U.S.-Iran hostilities send bitcoin lower even as ETF flows show demand
item.description: Resurgent U.S.-Iran hostilities send bitcoin lower even as ETF flows show demand So what: Review the source directly; automated analysis unavailable. [single source]

item.title: Bank of Thailand audits high-volume stablecoin trades to crack down on illicit finance: report
item.description: Bank of Thailand audits high-volume stablecoin trades to crack down on illicit finance: report So what: Review the source directly; automated analysis unavailable. [single source]

item.title: The Funding: Why Japan’s SBI Holdings is investing big in crypto
item.description: The Funding: Why Japan’s SBI Holdings is investing big in crypto So what: Review the source directly; automated analysis unavailable. [single source]

item.title: Circular to Licensed Corporations, SFC-licensed Virtual Asset Service Providers and Associated Entities - Anti-Money Laundering / Counter-Financing of Terrorism (1) FATF Statement on High-Risk Jurisdictions subject to a Call for Action (2) FATF Statement on Jurisdictions under Increased Monitoring (3) Outcomes from the FATF Plenary, 17-19 June 2026
item.description: Circular to Licensed Corporations, SFC-licensed Virtual Asset Service Providers and Associated Entities - Anti-Money Laundering / Counter-Financing of Terrorism (1) FATF Statement on High-Risk Jurisdictions subject to a Call for Action (2) FATF Statement on Jurisdictions under Increased Monitoring (3) Outcomes from the FATF Plenary, 17-19 June 2026 So what: Review the source directly; automated analysis unavailable. [official]

item.title: Ethereum Foundation privacy team spins out as for-profit EthSystems to serve institutions with Lubin, Bitmine backing
item.description: Ethereum Foundation privacy team spins out as for-profit EthSystems to serve institutions with Lubin, Bitmine backing So what: Review the source directly; automated analysis unavailable. [single source]

item.title: US-UK transatlantic taskforce unveils digital asset roadmap promoting stablecoin innovation
item.description: US-UK transatlantic taskforce unveils digital asset roadmap promoting stablecoin innovation So what: Review the source directly; automated analysis unavailable. [single source]

item.title: Binance bets on becoming a crypto 'super app' as stablecoins reshape growth
item.description: Binance bets on becoming a crypto 'super app' as stablecoins reshape growth So what: Review the source directly; automated analysis unavailable. [single source]

item.title: Wall Street turns cautious on Circle as analysts warn USDC economics face mounting pressure
item.description: Wall Street turns cautious on Circle as analysts warn USDC economics face mounting pressure So what: Review the source directly; automated analysis unavailable. [single source]

item.title: Mizuho downgrades Circle to underperform, cuts price target to $50 on Open USD threat

item.title: Wikipedia blackout could hurt how AI engines like ChatGPT understand crypto
